subject: Union dues reconciliation - June report
body:
Kevin,

Attaching the June dues reconciliation for Eastbridge. 3 members are still showing as non-payroll-deduct. I've flagged them with asterisks.

Can you cross-check against the BPS payroll export before the citywide meeting?

David

subject: Chapter newsletter - June 2026
body:
BEU Eastbridge Members,

June newsletter draft. Key items:
- Contract campaign update: 47 of 62 Eastbridge members have signed
- Dec 17 rally: City Hall Plaza, 4:30pm - PLEASE ATTEND
- Grievance update: Hall duty case filed, waiting on district response
- End of year reminder: Grade submission deadline June 20

Stay strong,
David Russo, Building Rep

subject: Apartment lease renewal - Nov 1 options
body:
Rachel,

The landlord sent the lease renewal terms. 4% rent increase, same 12-month term starting November 1.

I think we stay. The commute to Eastbridge on the Red Line is hard to beat. Worth discussing tonight.

Love, D

subject: Lexington and Concord trip logistics - Nov 7
body:
Teresa and Elena,

Here are the logistics for the November 7 Lexington and Concord day trip:
- Bus departs at 7:15am from the front entrance
- Permission slips due by Friday. I still have 4 outstanding
- Cost: $18 per student covered by History Club budget
- Return by 4:30pm

Let me know if either of you needs a copy of the liability waiver.

David

subject: AP grade reports - section summaries
body:
Teresa,

Quick one. Grade reports are due the 23rd. I'll have Period 2 and 3 APUSH section means in the shared spreadsheet by Tuesday. Holler if you need the AP curve notes before then.

David

subject: Parent conference slots - need Scotty's input
body:
Scott,

Mom mentioned you might want to drop by Eastbridge for the parent conference night to say hi. I'm running 3:30-6pm slots on November 18.

Let me know if you want to swing by. I'll save a slot at 5:30 so you're not waiting.

David

subject: Hall duty grievance - supplemental documentation
body:
Nathan,

Attaching the supplemental documentation for the Eastbridge hall duty grievance per your request at the LMC pre-meeting.

Included:
- Daily schedule showing the additional 4 duty periods beyond the contract maximum
- Email from Eastbridge principal dated April 3 mandating the policy change
- Statements from 3 affected members

Let me know if you need anything else before the June 25 meeting.

David Russo, BEU Eastbridge Building Rep

subject: Grievance draft for your review
body:
Tom,

Can you take a quick look at this grievance draft and tell me if the framing is clear? Looking for a second set of eyes on whether the timeline is logically presented before we file.

Thanks for the read.

David

subject: APUSH grade report - Period 3 and 5 curve
body:
Teresa and Elena,

Here are the AP curve notes for Period 3 and 5. I used a +4 points curve on the last DBQ based on the College Board scoring guidance.

Section means are in the shared spreadsheet. Grade reports are due the 23rd.

David

subject: PSLF - Mom's question about payment count
body:
Marie,

Mom asked me about PSLF again. I'm at 87 qualifying payments now, so 33 more to go. That's about 3 more years if I stay at BPS.

Tell her the loans will be gone by 2028 if everything stays on track. I'll explain at Sunday dinner if she wants more details.

David

subject: Building rep meeting notes - Dec 10
body:
Kevin,

Draft notes from today's rep meeting:
- Petition update: 47/62 Eastbridge signatures, 8 up from last week
- Rally confirmed: Dec 17, City Hall Plaza, 4:30pm
- Grievance: hall duty case filed Nov 26, no district response yet
- New members: 2 new sign-ups since October

Let me know if I missed anything before I send to members.

David

subject: Sunday plans
body:
Hey,

Frank's doing Sunday dinner at the parents'. 2pm. I said we'd bring dessert. Thinking the chocolate ricotta cake from Modern Pastry? Or we could do a fruit tart.

You around Sunday? Let me know.

D

subject: Sharing the labor history unit - open source
body:
Hello APUSH colleagues,

I've been building an open-source labor history unit for AP US History and wanted to share it here. It covers the Triangle Shirtwaist Fire, Lawrence Mill Strike, Pullman Strike, and 1919 Boston Police Strike.

All materials are free on GitHub: github.com/drusso-apush/labor-history-unit

Feedback very welcome, especially on the primary source scaffolding.

David Russo
Eastbridge High School, Boston Public Schools

subject: Re: AP curriculum pilot notes - Dr. Vale
body:
Angela,

Thanks for the notes from Dr. Vale's curriculum pilot session. The recommendation to add more DBQ scaffolding in Unit 4 aligns with what I've been seeing on student submissions.

I'll incorporate it into the next version of the resource vault materials.

David

subject: Re: LMC agenda - preparing for hall duty response
body:
Nathan,

I've reviewed the agenda for June 25. I want to make sure we have strong documentation in hand for the hall duty item.

I'll bring:
- Original grievance filing (filed Nov 26)
- Contract language (Article 20, Section 3)
- Timeline of the policy implementation
- Statements from 3 affected members

Is there anything specific the district has said they'll present?

David

subject: Apartment lease - heads up it's renewing Nov 1
body:
Frank,

Just letting you know our lease is up for renewal November 1. We're likely staying. Can't beat the Red Line access for my commute to Eastbridge.

Landlord wants a 4% increase. It's a little annoying but still reasonable for Dorchester.

David

subject: Re: AP labor unit - draft 3 feedback
body:
Teresa and Elena,

Thanks for the feedback on draft 3. I've tightened the 1870-1920 section and added the Lawrence textile strike primary sources as you suggested.

Let me know if the scope still fits within three weeks. If the Lawrence material is too much I can move it to an enrichment section.

David

subject: Mom's birthday gift ideas
body:
Frank and Marie,

Mom's birthday is coming up. I'm thinking we all chip in for a nice dinner out on the North End. Lucia's or somewhere she hasn't been.

Let me know if you have another idea. Rachel suggested a gift card to the Italian pottery place she likes in Providence but I think she'd prefer an experience.

David

subject: Re: Dues reconciliation - 3 flagged members
body:
Kevin,

I've attached the updated reconciliation with the 3 flagged members highlighted. Two of them started in January and may have had their payroll deduction set up late. The third one I'm not sure about. Can you check against the BPS payroll export?

David

subject: History Club primary source Friday - Biscuit update
body:
Scotty,

History Club Friday at 3pm. We're doing a primary source deep dive on the Revolution: pamphlets and broadsides. I've got enough copies for 12 kids.

Also, Biscuit had his vet checkup Tuesday. All good, they just want us to keep his weight steady. Vet said he looks great for a 3-year-old rescue mix.

David
